task_completion.description: Verify that the TablePress plugin is installed and activated in WordPress.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'TablePress' in the left-hand sidebar of the WordPress admin dashboard or listed as active on the Plugins page.

task_completion.description: Verify that the meteorite CSV data was imported into TablePress.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the TablePress 'Import' screen or a TablePress data view showing rows of meteorite data (e.g., containing the keyword 'Aachen').

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a new post with the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress post editor with the title exactly matching 'Historical Meteorite Landings Database'.

task_completion.description: Verify that an introductory sentence is included in the post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for standard paragraph text written in the post editor body, placed before the table.

task_completion.description: Verify that the TablePress table is embedded in the post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a TablePress block or a TablePress shortcode (e.g., [table id=1 /]) in the WordPress post editor.

task_completion.description: Verify the post is published and displays the interactive table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the published post on the front-end of the website showing the title, the introductory text, and the rendered interactive table (which should include search/pagination controls and meteorite data like 'Aachen').

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the web browser to interact with WordPress rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser open to the WordPress admin interface. There should be no terminal windows showing WP-CLI commands or direct database SQL inserts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used TablePress to embed the table, rather than just pasting text or using the default WordPress table block.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for TablePress-specific UI elements, shortcodes, or the distinct TablePress interactive data table (with 'Search' and 'Show entries' dropdowns) on the published page.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent imported the actual CSV file rather than manually typing a few fake rows.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table shown in TablePress or the published post should have many rows of authentic data (e.g., showing 'Aachen' and other meteorites), consistent with a real dataset import.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected post title is exactly 'Historical Meteorite Landings Database'. The CSV file is located at '/home/ga/Documents/meteorite_landings.csv'. The imported table data must contain the keyword 'Aachen' (an 1880 meteorite fall) to confirm the authentic NASA dataset was successfully imported via TablePress.
